Kids Profile.description: Restricted to Kids Hub content only. Simplified UI. No access to Live TV, account settings, or mature content. One-size-fits-all for ages 2-12 with no further granularity. A preschooler sees the same content catalog as an 11-year-old.
Kids Profile.notes: Only option is Kids toggle at profile creation. Cannot further restrict to TV-Y only (preschool) or expand to PG-13. Unlike Netflix (Little Kids vs Older Kids), Hulu has a single Kids tier.

Under 13 Standard.description: Standard profile with birthdate indicating under 13. R-rated and TV-MA content automatically blocked. User sees standard UI but with restricted content catalog. Has access to search and full navigation unlike Kids profile.
Under 13 Standard.notes: Determined by birthdate at profile creation. Cannot be changed by parent without contacting Hulu support to modify birth year. Similar rating access to Kids profile but with standard (non-restricted) UI.

Under 17 Standard.description: Standard profile with birthdate indicating 13-16. R-rated and TV-MA content blocked. Note: reports indicate 17-year-old profiles CAN access TV-MA content, so the exact cutoff may be 17 rather than 18.
Under 17 Standard.notes: Birthdate-based restriction. Hulu does not clearly document the exact age-to-rating mapping. Testing has shown inconsistencies (e.g., 17-year-old profiles accessing TV-MA). The PIN (if enabled) is required to access this profile.

Adult Standard (17+).description: Full content access. No restrictions. Includes FX on Hulu mature content, add-on channel content (STARZ, Max), and full Live TV channel access (if subscribed). PIN required to access if PIN protection is enabled.
Adult Standard (17+).notes: Default for profiles with birthdate indicating 17 or older. The only protection is account-wide PIN which gates access to all non-Kids profiles.

content_descriptors.notes: Hulu does not expose content descriptors (violence, language, nudity, etc.) as filterable categories. Content ratings (MPAA, TV) are the only filtering mechanism, and they are not configurable by the parent -- determined solely by profile type (Kids) or birthdate (Standard). Unlike Netflix which displays descriptors on title pages, Hulu's descriptor display is inconsistent.

fx_on_hulu.description: FX on Hulu is a branded content hub featuring original series from FX Networks. The majority of FX content is rated TV-MA and includes graphic violence, explicit language, sexual content, and drug use.
notable_series: What We Do in the Shadows
fx_on_hulu.filtering: Blocked on Kids profiles and under-17 standard profiles. No separate toggle to block FX content specifically.

live_tv_ratings.description: Hulu + Live TV subscribers have access to 95+ live channels. Live broadcasts carry standard TV Parental Guidelines ratings.
live_tv_ratings.filtering_behavior: Kids profiles block ALL live TV access. Non-Kids profiles have unrestricted live TV access regardless of age-based restrictions. This is a significant gap.

ad_content_gap.description: On ad-supported Hulu plans, advertisements are NOT filtered by profile maturity rating. A child watching a TV-PG show can see ad trailers for TV-MA horror movies or adult dramas. These ads cannot be skipped immediately.
ad_content_gap.mitigation: Subscribe to ad-free plan ($18.99/mo or $99.99/mo with Live TV)

MPAA.notes: Full MPAA coverage for films. Includes NR (Not Rated) for unrated content. Applied automatically based on profile type/birthdate.

TV_Parental_Guidelines.notes: Full TV rating coverage for on-demand series. Live TV broadcasts carry these ratings but filtering is limited for non-Kids profiles.

ESRB.notes: No gaming content on Hulu. Not applicable.

PEGI.notes: Not applicable. Hulu is US-only (separate service in Japan).

Common_Sense_Media.notes: Hulu does not display or integrate Common Sense Media ratings.

key_limitations: Binary maturity model: Kids profile or birthdate-based Standard. No configurable maturity slider.
Birthdate-based restrictions cannot be changed by parent (requires Hulu support for birth year changes).
No per-title blocking capability of any kind.
Ads on ad-supported plans are NOT filtered by profile maturity.
Kids profile is one-size-fits-all: a 3-year-old sees the same content as an 11-year-old.
Exact age-to-rating mapping is undocumented and has reported inconsistencies.
Kids profiles block Live TV entirely (safe but limiting).
Non-Kids profiles have unrestricted Live TV access regardless of age setting.
Account-wide PIN (not per-profile) is a single point of failure.
Hulu standalone app shutting down in 2026; migrating to Disney+.
